step_by_step_thinking:
From the articles reviewed, we understand that heroin is derived from morphine after undergoing acetylation reactions. It eventually breaks down within the body back to morphine before interacting with opioid receptors. Thus, heroin itself doesn't contain aspirin (ASA), pentazocine, propoxyphene; rather, it is transformed to morphine and potentially 6-acetylmorphine during metabolism.
\boxed{B} Based on the information extracted from the medical references, heroin specifically goes through metabolic pathways leading to either 6-monoacetyl morphine or directly transforming into morphine upon entering the system — none of which includes ASA, pentazocine, or propoxyphene components.
